Name: spectral_perturbation_robustness
Title: Spectral Perturbation Robustness: Training-Free Inference with PAC-Bayes Bounds and Latency-Aware Optimization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that spectral perturbation analysis can be used to develop training-free inference methods that provide robustness guarantees with PAC-Bayes bounds and latency-aware optimization, enabling efficient and reliable deployment in resource-constrained environments.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in training-free machine learning, such as Spectral Latent Steering, Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation, and Spectral Quantization Routing. However, our approach focuses on spectral perturbation analysis and its application to inference robustness, providing a novel perspective on the intersection of PAC-Bayes theory and latency-aware optimization.
Abstract: We propose a novel training-free inference framework that leverages spectral perturbation analysis to provide robustness guarantees with PAC-Bayes bounds and latency-aware optimization. Our approach, Spectral Perturbation Robustness, restricts test-time optimization to a low-rank, norm-preserving subspace, ensuring strict computational budgets while preserving the optimization landscape's geometry. We theoretically ground our method in information-theoretic bounds, demonstrating that low-rank projections capture the majority of gradient-aligned signal required for reasoning improvement. Empirically, we evaluate our approach on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, showing that it matches or exceeds full-space perturbation baselines while reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead and enforcing deterministic latency bounds.
Experiments: Evaluate the performance of Spectral Perturbation Robustness on mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks
Compare the results with full-space perturbation baselines and other training-free inference methods
Assess the impact of spectral perturbation analysis on inference robustness and latency-aware optimization
Investigate the application of PAC-Bayes bounds to provide robustness guarantees for our approach
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of spectral perturbation analysis in capturing the underlying geometry of the optimization landscape
The potential for overfitting or underfitting in the low-rank subspace
The need for careful tuning of hyperparameters to balance robustness and computational efficiency
